result.stdout:
USAGE: monkey [--help] [test] [--version] [<subcommand> [<options>]]

SUBCOMMANDS:

    build <options>       Build Monkey (.mk) files.
    new <options>         Create a new Monkey project.
    run <options>         Run Monkey files.

    Use 'monkey <subcommand> --help' for additional information.

OPTIONS:

    test                      --version, -v         Display toolchain and language versions.
    --help                display this list of options.

commands.payload:
USAGE: monkey [--help] [test] [--version] [<subcommand> [<options>]]

SUBCOMMANDS:

    build <options>       Build Monkey (.mk) files.
    new <options>         Create a new Monkey project.
    run <options>         Run Monkey files.

    Use 'monkey <subcommand> --help' for additional information.

OPTIONS:

    test                      --version, -v         Display toolchain and language versions.
    --help                display this list of options.

result.stdout:
USAGE: monkey build [--help] [--verbosity <verbosity>] [--outputdir <output>]
                    [--target <integrated|dotnet>] [--warningsaserrors]
                    [<project>]

PROJECT:

    <project>             The '*mkproj' file to operate on. If a file is not
                          specified, the command will search the current
                          directory for one.

OPTIONS:

    --verbosity, -v <verbosity>
                          Set the MSBuild verbosity level. Allowed values are
                          q[uiet], m[inimal], n[ormal], d[etailed], and
                          diag[nostic].
    --outputdir, -o <output>
                          The output directory to place built artifacts in.
    --target, -t <integrated|dotnet>
                          The platform to target.
    --warningsaserrors, -strict
                          Flag. Emits warnings as errors.
    --help                display this list of options.

result.stdout:
USAGE: monkey new [--help] [--name <string>] [--output <string>]
                  [--template <string>] [--verbose] [--initgit]

OPTIONS:

    --name, -n <string>   The name of the project.
    --output, -o <string> The output directory of the generated project files.
    --template, -t <string>
                          The application template.
    --verbose, -v         Flag. Emits extra generation information.
    --initgit             Flag. Initializes git and creation of .gitignore,
                          .git/* and .github/* files.
    --help                display this list of options.
